response.justification: The candidate is a highly experienced full-stack engineer (15+ years) with strong web, Node.js/TypeScript, and DLang backgrounds, but his profile does not match this role's core requirement of deep, current C++ expertise for industrial automation. His only professional C++ experience was a brief stint at National Instruments in 2014–2015, and he shows no exposure to MQTT, message brokers in an industrial context, IEC 62443/SDL practices, or OT protocols. While his DLang low-latency systems work demonstrates transferable concurrency and performance skills, the gap on the primary stack and domain is too large for a senior C++ leadership position.
strengths: Extensive overall engineering experience across web, embedded, and high-performance systems
Low-latency, asynchronous, high-throughput systems work (DLang at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic) shows strong concurrency fundamentals
Demonstrated mentorship and knowledge-sharing (workshops, code katas, testing courses) aligns with the mentoring responsibility
concerns: Minimal professional C++ experience — a single ~6-month role in 2014–2015, with no recent C++/STL work
No industrial automation, IoT protocol (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA), or IEC 62443 / SDL security background
Recent career trajectory is web/full-stack and AI tooling, diverging from the on-prem industrial systems focus of this role

key_factors.factor: No industrial cybersecurity or OT protocol experience
